what_it_does: Defines a global toast notification bridge on window, registers a React Toast component as a listener, shows a message briefly, and exposes the component as window.Toast.
observations: Assigns window.dsaToast to broadcast a message to every registered listener.
Toast stores the active message in local state.
Toast registers a sub listener on mount and removes it on cleanup.
Each received message is cleared with a 2200 ms timeout.
The rendered toast includes a ViShape icon and a span containing the current message.
standouts: Uses window-scoped pub-sub for notification delivery.
Combines global dispatcher setup and React component definition in one file.
The toast visibility is controlled by toggling the show class based on message presence.
conventions: Uses aliased React hooks destructured from the global React object.
Uses a global window export pattern for both dsaToast and Toast.
Uses JSX with className interpolation for conditional state styling.
Assumes ViShape is available in scope.
concerns: Timeouts are not cleared on unmount or when a later message replaces an earlier one.
Multiple mounted Toast instances would each register a listener and display independently.
Global window mutation can collide with other code using the same property names.
The message payload is rendered directly without local type checks.
notes: This file is a compact global toast bridge and display component using window-level listener registration.
questions: Is Toast intended to be mounted exactly once?
Should overlapping toast messages reset or cancel prior clear timers?
What payload types are expected for dsaToast messages?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the implementation's global notifier shape, though single-instance usage is assumed rather than enforced in this file.
intention_signals: It seems intended to provide a lightweight app-wide notification path without props or context by exposing a global function that drives a shared toast UI.
novelty: A global window.dsaToast dispatcher and a self-registering React toast component are colocated in a small file.
contribution: A compact global toast notification substrate with a window-level dispatch function, listener lifecycle wiring, timed message clearing, and a fixed toast UI shell with icon and text.
summary_for_embedding: This file defines a window-backed React toast notifier that listens for global dsaToast messages and displays them briefly in a styled toast element.
intention_for_embedding: This was for simple app-wide transient notifications through an imperative global trigger and a shared visual toast component.

symbols.does: Renders the toast UI and subscribes to global toast messages.
symbols.description: Toast keeps the current message in React state, registers a listener with window._toastListeners on mount, and removes that listener on cleanup. When a message arrives it displays the message, schedules it to clear after 2200 ms, and renders a dsa-toast container with a ViShape icon and message span.

symbols.does: Receives a toast message, stores it, and schedules it to be cleared.
symbols.description: sub is the listener function registered by Toast with the global listener array. It updates Toast state with the incoming message and starts a timeout that resets the message to null after 2200 ms.
